steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The blank sits between two complete independent clauses. The first is “A subseasonal weather forecast attempts to predict weather conditions three to four weeks in advance.” The second is “its predictions are therefore more short-term than those of the seasonal forecast, which attempts to predict the weather more than a month in advance”—beginning with the conjunctive adverb “therefore.”

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: A semicolon separates two independent clauses, and choice C provides the correct punctuation. A comma alone (A) creates a comma splice, no punctuation (B) leaves the sentence fused, and “and” (D) mismatches the causal relationship “therefore” signals. That matches choice C — C is correct.
